alignments.preprint_claim_text: We have developed a collaborative filtering model that makes accurate predictions of individuals' preferences. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The predicted preferences can be inferred with an expectation-maximization algorithm whose running time scales linearly per iteration. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.rationale: The publication clarifies the complexity statement by specifying scaling with the number of observed ratings, matching the original per-iteration linear scaling claim.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The approach does not assume that ratings depend linearly on a measure of similarity. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: This conclusion appears in the publication but has no counterpart in the preprint claims list; it is an added clarification about model assumptions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method assesses both the quality of metadata and the performance of community detection algorithms simultaneously.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The quality of metadata is evaluated based on its ability to predict edge placements in the network rather than its alignment with network communities.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method can predict connections to missing nodes and missing metadata using only the available metadata.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method can predict connections to missing nodes when only the metadata are available.
alignments.rationale: Both claims match on predicting connections from metadata; the publication frames this with higher certainty and omits explicit mention of 'missing metadata' prediction, suggesting a stronger asserted result for node links.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: While exact agreements between metadata tokens and inferred data groups are rare, the metadata is still informative of the network structure.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The metadata, while not perfectly aligned with the inferred data groups, are often informative of the network structure.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same observation about imperfect alignment but persistent informativeness of metadata with equivalent meaning and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: For all constant $k \ge 2$, the runtime $T$ follows a distribution close to the geometric one with success probability equal to the probability to flip between $1$ and $k$ bits divided by the size of the plateau.

alignments.rationale: The publication gives the expected-runtime formula but drops the explicit conclusion that it 'only depends' on that probability (no mention of independence from other mutation characteristics), so the claim is modified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The optimal mutation rate for standard bit mutation is approximately $k/(en)$.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Current debates on the co-evolution of society and technology can be enriched by a proper appreciation of the long history of the ideas involved.
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about enriching current debates is identical in preprint and publication.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The problem of extracting missing attribute values is formalized as a sequence tagging task using a joint model with recurrent neural networks and Conditional Random Fields.

alignments.pub_claim_text: A novel attention mechanism is developed to provide interpretable explanations for the model's decisions.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Neural network-based algorithms provide a promising approach to jet classification problems, such as boosted top jet tagging.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The jet observable defined by the CNN obeys the canonical definition of infrared safety.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The jet observable defined by the CNN obeys the canonical definition of infrared safety.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The jet observable is unaffected by the presence of the extra gluon, as long as it is soft or collinear with one of the quarks.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The CNN tagger is robust with respect to possible mis-modeling of soft and collinear final-state radiation by Monte Carlo generators.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The CNN tagger is robust with respect to possible mis-modeling of soft and collinear final-state radiation by Monte Carlo generators.

alignments.pub_claim_text: BindsNET is a new Python package designed for the simulation of spiking neural networks, specifically aimed at machine learning and reinforcement learning.

alignments.pub_claim_text: BindsNET enables rapid building and simulation of spiking networks with user-friendly, concise syntax.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The device can be reconfigured to trade off resource utilization with training time to fit networks and datasets of varying sizes.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The device can be reconfigured to balance resource utilization with training time for different networks and datasets.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The combined effects of complexity reduction and easy reconfigurability enable significantly greater exploration of network hyperparameters and structures on-chip.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The combined effects of complexity reduction and reconfigurability facilitate greater exploration of network hyperparameters and structures on-chip.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The usage of image-to-image translation models allows for the translation of labeled RGB data to synthetic TIR data.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The average accuracy for the emotion recognition system using MFCC features is 59.25%. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Combining MFCC and epoch features improves the recognition performance to 64.2%. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.rationale: The conceptual conclusion is the same in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Bayesian machine learning techniques can improve predictions of nuclear binding energies by utilizing deviations between experimental and calculated masses.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Bayesian machine-learning techniques significantly improve the quality of model-based predictions of nuclear properties of rare isotopes far from stability.
alignments.rationale: The publication shifts emphasis from a general statement about binding-energy prediction to a specific focus on rare isotopes/extrapolation, revising and narrowing the claimed application domain.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Gaussian processes provide a better and more stable performance in reducing rms deviation from experimental masses compared to Bayesian neural networks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Gaussian processes provide a better and more stable performance in reducing the root-mean-square deviation from experimental results compared to Bayesian neural networks.
alignments.rationale: The publication reiterates the direct performance comparison between Gaussian processes and BNNs with equivalent meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The resulting rms deviations from experiment on the testing dataset are comparable to those of more phenomenological models.
alignments.rationale: No publication claim restates the preprint's specific comparison to phenomenological models; the publication instead emphasizes other extrapolation and impact points, so this specific claim is omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The empirical coverage probability curves obtained match reference values, ensuring the honesty of uncertainty quantification.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The empirical coverage probability curves obtained from the proposed method match reference values well, ensuring honesty in uncertainty quantification.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the empirical coverage / honesty of UQ claim with essentially the same content.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We derive an adversarial algorithm for the problem of approximating an unknown quantum pure state.
alignments.pub_claim_text: We derive an adversarial algorithm for the problem of approximating an unknown quantum pure state.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the derivation claim verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The adversarial formulation enables us to execute the algorithm on near-term quantum computers.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The adversarial formulation enables us to execute the algorithm on near-term quantum computers.
alignments.rationale: The claim about near-term implementability is preserved unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Resilient backpropagation algorithms perform remarkably well in optimizing the two circuits.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Resilient backpropagation algorithms perform remarkably well in optimizing the two circuits.
alignments.rationale: Performance observation is restated identically.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We use the bipartite entanglement entropy to design an efficient heuristic for the stopping criterion.
alignments.pub_claim_text: We use the bipartite entanglement entropy to design an efficient heuristic for the stopping criterion.
alignments.rationale: The heuristic-design claim is unchanged between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our approach may find application in quantum state tomography.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our approach may find application in quantum state tomography.
alignments.rationale: Both versions present this as a tentative potential application with similar hedging.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The anisotropic diffusion model can be effectively used for automatic detection of neurons in histological sections of the adult human brain cortex.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method for automatic detection of neurons in histological sections achieves an average correlation of 88.79% with human experts.
alignments.rationale: The publication replaces the general effectiveness claim with a quantified performance metric (88.79% correlation), clarifying the result and somewhat tempering the broad qualitative assertion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method has correctly distinguished over 95% of neuron bodies in test data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method correctly distinguished 95.41% of neurons in the test images.
alignments.rationale: The publication refines the preprint's >95% figure to a precise 95.41%, clarifying and slightly strengthening the numerical claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method operates in a time much shorter than other comparable methods.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method is significantly faster than human raters and other automatic methods.
alignments.rationale: The publication quantifies and emphasizes the speed advantage ("significantly faster"), clarifying and amplifying the preprint's timing claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The approach in model training and evaluation addresses variability among human experts regarding the golden standard for neuron and cell counting.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method provides consistent and reproducible results across all sections.
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes expert-variability mitigation as consistent and reproducible performance across sections, clarifying the original claim about handling inter-expert variability.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A joint illumination-deconvolution scheme is designed to overcome diffraction-photons in imaging. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A joint illumination-deconvolution scheme is designed to overcome diffraction-photons in imaging. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The preprint and publication statements are effectively identical in contribution and certainty, so there is no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The designed scheme enables the acquisition of intensity and depth images despite the presence of diffraction-photons. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The designed scheme enables the acquisition of intensity and depth images. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same result (acquisition of intensity and depth images) with equivalent certainty; any wording difference is minor.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A proof-of-concept experiment demonstrates the viability of the designed scheme. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A proof-of-concept experiment demonstrates the viability of the designed scheme. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The experiment claim is carried over verbatim with the same certainty, indicating no change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Co-designing the illumination and deconvolution phases of imaging can substantially overcome diffraction-photons. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Co-designing the illumination and deconvolution phases of imaging can substantially overcome diffraction-photons. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion appears verbatim in the publication with the same level of certainty and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed memory augmented neural network architecture serves as a language-agnostic text normalization system and avoids unacceptable errors made by LSTM-based recurrent neural networks. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed memory augmented neural network architecture serves as a language-agnostic text normalization system. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: This novel architecture reduces the frequency of unacceptable errors compared to LSTM-based recurrent neural networks. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed system requires significantly lesser amounts of data, training time, and compute resources. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed system requires significantly lesser amounts of data, training time, and compute resources. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This resource-efficiency claim appears unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Data up-sampling can improve the performance of the text normalization system by providing sufficient examples in semiotic classes. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Data up-sampling can improve the performance of the text normalization system by providing sufficient examples in semiotic classes. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about up-sampling is carried over with the same wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Memory augmented networks with meta-learning capabilities can lead to a superior text normalization system despite some remaining errors. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Memory augmented networks with meta-learning capabilities can lead to a superior text normalization system despite some remaining errors. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The concluding claim about memory-augmented/meta-learning benefits is unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed algorithm for blind separation of single-channel audio signals demonstrates high-quality separation results when model assumptions are satisfied and instruments are clearly distinguishable. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed algorithm for blind separation of single-channel audio signals demonstrates high-quality separation results when model assumptions are satisfied and instruments are clearly distinguishable. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The result statement is effectively identical between versions and retains the same conditions and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The novel sparse pursuit algorithm can match discrete frequency spectra from recorded signals with continuous spectra delivered by the parametric model. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The novel sparse pursuit algorithm can match discrete frequency spectra from recorded signals with continuous spectra delivered by the parametric model. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This algorithmic capability is stated the same way in the publication with matching certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method is especially suitable for dealing with spectra from acoustic instruments due to its pitch-invariance and minimal hyperparameter requirements. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method is especially suitable for dealing with spectra from acoustic instruments due to its pitch-invariance and minimal hyperparameter requirements. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The suitability claim for acoustic instruments is preserved verbatim, with the same level of certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The dictionary constructed for one recording can be applied to a different recording with similar instruments without additional training. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The dictionary constructed for one recording can be applied to a different recording with similar instruments without additional training. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The cross-recording applicability of the dictionary is present in both versions; the publication frames it as a conclusion rather than a raw result, but the substance is the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Combinations of instruments with similar spectral characteristics pose a conceptual difficulty for the algorithm. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The presence of inharmonicity in the model can impede the performance of the sparse pursuit algorithm. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Incremental learning can be a potential solution for training deep learning models in medical image segmentation. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Incremental learning can be a potential solution for training deep learning models in medical image segmentation. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: It is essential to keep some 'important' images and annotations from the past to preserve collective information during incremental learning. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: It is essential to keep some 'important' images and annotations from the past to preserve collective information during incremental learning. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The necessity of retaining representative past data is stated identically and with the same certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed framework for applying incremental learning demonstrates increased learning capacity when using the novel methods for selecting representative data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed framework for applying incremental learning demonstrates increased learning capacity when using representative data selection methods. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The methods were evaluated in different scenarios using MR images. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The methods were evaluated in different scenarios using MR images. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: $q$-neurons are a new generic type of stochastic neurons that utilize activation functions based on Jackson's $q$-derivatives. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Experimental results demonstrate consistently improved performances of $q$-neurons over state-of-the-art standard activation functions. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Classical video description approaches cannot cope with the diversity in unconstrained open domain videos.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Existing datasets lack adequate visual diversity and complexity of linguistic structures.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Existing datasets do not contain adequate visual diversity or complexity of linguistic structures.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the dataset limitation verbatim but with higher stated certainty, so the claim is unchanged semantically but with amplified confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The paper surveys state of the art approaches with a focus on deep learning models.
alignments.rationale: The publication's claims do not mention the paper being a survey or that contribution; this claim is not present in the published claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Analysis of video description models is challenging due to difficulties in ascertaining contributions of visual features and language models.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Video description research is still in its infancy due to challenges in analyzing models and contributions towards accuracy.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Multi-scale feature extraction and multi-level feature representation are employed to enhance feature capture and fusion.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not restate these specific architectural contributions in its high-level claims, so they appear omitted from the published summary.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Resistive Processing Unit (RPU) devices can provide significant power and speed benefits when training recurrent neural networks (RNNs).
alignments.pub_claim_text: Resistive processing unit (RPU) devices can provide significant power and speed benefits when training recurrent neural networks (RNNs) such as LSTMs.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Asymmetry in updates for RNNs results in an increase in the test error compared to the ideal case trained with floating point numbers.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A stochastic rounding scheme can reduce the input signal resolution for successful training from 7 bits to 5 bits.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The input signal resolution to the device arrays needs to be at least 7 bits for successful training, but a stochastic rounding scheme can reduce this requirement to 5 bits.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: RPU device variations and hardware noise can mitigate overfitting, reducing the need for using dropout.
alignments.pub_claim_text: RPU device variations and hardware noise can mitigate overfitting, reducing the need for using dropout.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this observation verbatim, so the claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The models trained are roughly 1500 times larger than the fully connected network trained on the MNIST dataset in terms of the total number of multiplication and summation operations performed per epoch.
alignments.rationale: The published claims do not include this specific quantitative model-size comparison, so this detailed result is omitted.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed distributed solutions guarantee convergence to the minimizer with a linear rate under strong convexity.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates this theoretical convergence result identically, so it is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study focuses on learning in scenarios with large datasets and large-dimensional feature spaces.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study focuses on learning in scenarios with large datasets and large-dimensional feature spaces.

alignments.rationale: The substantive claim is the same but the publication reduces the stated certainty (high -> medium), so the claim is presented with tempered confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Some versions of hacking intervals are equivalent to classical confidence intervals, providing a more intuitive interpretation. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Some versions of hacking intervals are equivalent to classical confidence intervals, offering a more intuitive interpretation. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The equivalence statement is retained with essentially the same certainty and interpretation, only minor wording changes occurred.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Hacking intervals are a new concept that provide a range of a summary statistic based on possible endogenous manipulations of the data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication includes an explicit definitional/result statement about hacking intervals that does not have a direct counterpart in the preprint, so it is treated as an added claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Generative adversarial training has been successfully extended to the quantum domain. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Generative adversarial training can be extended to the quantum domain using quantum circuits. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The preprint asserted successful extension; the publication reframes this as a capability ('can be extended') using quantum circuits, which weakens the original success claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Quantum generative adversarial networks can be trained successfully using quantum circuits. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Quantum generative adversarial networks can be trained successfully as demonstrated by a simple numerical experiment. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the success claim but clarifies that it is supported by a simple numerical experiment, adding information about the evidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Gradients, a key element in generative adversarial network training, can be computed using a quantum circuit. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Gradients, a key element in generative adversarial network training, can be computed using a quantum circuit. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The computational claim about gradients is stated identically; only the categorical label changes, not the claim content or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The performance of projective simulation (PS) and standard learning approaches are qualitatively and quantitatively similar. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The performance of projective simulation and standard learning approaches are qualitatively and quantitatively similar. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same performance comparison wording and level of certainty, indicating no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: It is much easier to choose optimal model parameters in the case of projective simulation, with a reduced computational effort of one to two orders of magnitude. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Choosing optimal model parameters is much easier in projective simulation, requiring reduced computational effort of one to two orders of magnitude. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same claim about parameter selection difficulty and computational savings with equivalent certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The projective simulation model stands out for its simplicity in terms of the number of model parameters. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The projective simulation model stands out for its simplicity in terms of the number of model parameters. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The claim text is the same, but the publication reports lower certainty (medium vs high), so the claim's confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Projective simulation allows for quantum mechanical generalization, which leads to a speed-up in deliberation time. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: This quantum-generalization / speed-up claim appears in the preprint but is absent from the publication's listed claims, indicating it was removed or de-emphasized.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The agent is able to reach the stairs and descend to the next level in 98% of cases. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The agent is able to reach the stairs and descend to the next level in 98% of cases. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The reported success rate is identical between preprint and publication with no change in framing or certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Rogue-like games serve as a very natural and challenging task for reinforcement learning. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Rogue-like games serve as a very natural and challenging task for reinforcement learning. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study exploits a version of A3C partitioned on different situations. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The use of a version of Asynchronous Advantage Actor-Critic (A3C) partitioned on different situations improves the agent's performance. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
